publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2020-09-17 18:06:11 -0400.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Collection includes personal and professional materials regarding his travels and work at Cornell

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Professor of Soil Science. Robert Demorest Miller grew up in Columbia, Mo., earned a B.S. from the University of Missouri in 1940, an M.S. from the University of Nebraska in 1941, and a Ph.D. from Cornell University in 1948. During 1944-1945, he served as an air traffic and air defense controller in the Pacific in the Army Air Corps. On August 28, 1945, he was a member of a small advance party sent to Atsugi Air Base near Tokyo to prepare for the major American landings beginning the occupation of Japan. Shortly after landing, he raised the first American flag over Japan, on the radio mast of a C-47, two days before American combat troops began the actual armed occupation. He wrote about his wartime experiences in the book: "Descent from Niitaka, 1941-1945: First Flag Over Japan." After the war, he worked at the University of California at Berkeley, returning to Cornell in 1952 where he remained until his retirement in 1987. From 1964 to 1965, he served as assistant to the Provost Dale Corson and from 1967 to 1971 as dean of the faculty, in which position he played a role in the peaceful resolution of the Cornell '69 crisis, remaining a lifelong friend of President James Perkins. He is considered by his colleagues to be a "Founding Father" of soil physics. Robert Miller died on April 11, 2011.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Collection includes personal and family materials including an "auto log" travel diary kept by Miller's mother Flora Grace Ernst Miller from the family's 1933-1934 trip to Germany; materials relating to Miller's World War II experiences and the preparation of his book about them titled Descent from Niitaka; a letter to the Committee on the Armed Services regarding his perspectives on gays in the military; correspondence as Dean of the Faculty and later (to 1975); Cornell '69 materials; scientific correspondence, 1975-2010; materials relating to trips to Russia, 1973 and 1982; letters from James Perkins; slides and photograph albums from scientific trips; and publications and conference papers. course preparation material; material, photos, and slides relating to trips to Takutsk, Moscow, and others; awards; also business correspondence; publications and reprints.

relatedmaterial.head: RELATED MATERIALS
relatedmaterial.p: See also the Robert D. Miller. Oral Histories, #13-6-1285 and Merritt Miller papers, #4583.

arrangement.head: SERIES LIST
arrangement.p: Series I. Cornell University Material and Correspondence Boxes 1, 6 Material and Correspondence on Cornell '69 James Perkins
Series II. Correspondence (Academic, Scientific, and Personal) Box 1, 2, 6

Series IV. Courses Box 3 Agronomy Course 607 R.D. Miller-Overheads-Soil Physics Part 1 Statics Agronomy Course 607 R.D. Miller-Overheads-Soil Physics Part 2 Dynamics Agronomy Course 607 R.D. Miller, Course Materials
Series V. Manuscripts and Lectures Box 3, 6
Series VI. Slides and Photos Box 3, 6 Yakutsk, Ice, Students Doctoral Students' Slides, Work, Scaling, RDM's Slides Other Slides
Series IX. Personal and Family Materials Box 4-7
Series X. World War II Materials Boxes 5, 6

list.head: Books Removed From Collection, Cataloged Separately:
list.item: Perkins, A. James. The University in A Restless Decade. New York, NY: The International Council for Educational Development, 1972.
Miller, D. Robert. Descent from Niitaka 1941-1945 First Flag over Japan. Ithaca, NY: Cayuga Press, 2002
Rossiter, S. Caleb. The Chimes of Freedom Flashing. Washington, DC.: TCA Press, 1996

Alexis Lawrence Romanoff. The University Campus. Ithaca, NY: The Cayuga Press, 1960, Inscribed by the author to the Miller family with laid in Romanoff business card and verse written for the Cornell Centennial.
The 1902 Cornellian the Book of the Junior Class. Cornell University, 1901

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Robert D. Miller papers, #21-13-3960. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

accessrestrict.head: Access Restrictions:
accessrestrict.p: Oral history may be restricted in part.See transcript for restrictions.

Germany -- Description and travel.
Soil science.

World War, 1939-1945 -- Personal narratives, American.
Airmen -- United States.
controlaccess.corpname: New York State College of Agriculture and Life Sciences. Department of Crop and Soil Sciences

did.unittitle: Series I. Cornell University Material and Correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains disks, photos, journals, and notes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cornell '69 Liberalism and the Crisis of the American University by Donald A. Downs, with comments by R.D. Miller

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Newspaper Clippings about '69 and Letter Correspondence with Cornell Faculties

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Chimes of Freedom Flashing by Caleb S. Rossiter. Removed and catalogued separately.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Transcript of Statement, Correspondence James Perkins (1995), and others

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes"A Failure to Communicate: James Perkins, COSEP, and Black Power at Cornell" Honors Thesis by Eric Rauchway and An Article by Keith R. Johnson

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Miscellany, contains calender (1967-1968),a thesis draft by unknown author, Cornell University Student Faculty Board on Student Conduct Notice of Hearing(1968), a photo, notes, and a letter from Davis (2003)

did.unittitle: Cornell '69 More Post-event Materials: Downs, Rossiter, others
Collection of Newspaper on the 20th Anniversary of the Willard Straight Hall Takeover and Speech Draft

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Newspaper and Journal Collection

did.unittitle: General Legislation of the University Faculty and Extracts from Its Records

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a private letter to his brothers and sisters describing his perspective on the situation, correspondence, clippings, Newsweek magazine, speeches, Henderson Commission information

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Transcript of an oral history interview conducted by Gould P. Colman as part of the Challenge to Governance Project. With index and correspondence regarding the oral history and its restrictions. See also archives 13-6-1285 for the audio tapes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes information and correspondence regarding the death of Perkins, Perkins prize, Ari Van Tienhoven Tom Jones, DVD-1764 of "Cornell 1969: Key Issues Then and Now. A University Faculty Forum, 3 May 1999, Kennedy Hall;" "The 30th Anniversary of WSH Takeover" panel discussion, Memorial Room, Willard Straight Hall, 19 April 1999, the DVD also includes relevant news clippings. Also a copy of a 1978 letter sent to Perkins from the President of Oberlin College Robert Fuller about increasing enrollment of black students that Perkins forwarded to Miller in 1995.

did.unittitle: Copy of Correspondence of James A. Perkins and Robert D. Miller
Correspondence between James A. Perkins and John R. Silber (President of Boston University), and Newspaper Clippings about Them
Invitations, Photos, and Pamphlets for James A. Perkins Prize for Interracial Understanding and Harmony
Series II. Correspondence (Academic, Scientific, and Personal)
Correspondence with Cornell University and Some Other Institutions
Scientific Correspondence with Dean of Faculty

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains Correspondence with Frank H.T. Rhodes and Alfred E. Kahn

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains Correspondence with Alfred E. Kahn

did.unittitle: Dean of the Faculty Correspondence (Not Related to Cornell '69) 1

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a sub-series of National Science Foundation

did.unittitle: Dean of the Faculty Correspondence (Not Related to Cornell '69) 2

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence from daughter Anne Miller, Herbert Cahoon of the Pierpont Morgan library and Morris Bishop regarding Anne's interest in witchcraft and desire to become a witch. Also correspondence regarding an amusing anecdote at Alice Cook's retirement involving David Burak and a joke about the Cornell mace.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence regarding Robert and Beulah's anniversary. Correspondence include Keith Kennedy, Dale Corson, and war buddy Captain Walter Huckbee.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence and clipping regarding his election to the position of Dean of Faculty from Royse Murphy and Rolph Fairchild. Also a thank you note to a Mrs. Burton (a figure from his childhood) from Miller describing his work. Also a note of congratulations on retirement from Marlin Cline.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters and photographs from Perkins, Corson, Rhodes. Also a program from the Cornell Centennial Dinner. A photograph of Perkins shaking hands with Tom Jones inscribed "Without Bob Miller's cool head there would be no handshake."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters from Miller's father Dean Merritt Miller and one to his wife.

did.unittitle: Correspondence to wife Beulah "Buty" from Russia

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters and ephemera sent to his wife while he was in Russia

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters from colleagues and grad students. Includes letter to Dale Corson on soil physics. Also a floppy disk (FD-340) of soil correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence Miller's trip to Moscow State University and Ershov's visit to Cornell. Includes photographs of Ershov and of Russian trip.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Scientific correspondence with Miller's brother Professor Edward E Miller of the University of Wisconsin.

did.unittitle: Robert D. Miller, Articles and Papers on 0°, 1
Robert D. Miller, Articles and Papers on 0°, 2

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes some slides

did.unittitle: Robert D. Miller, Articles and Papers on 0°, 3
Robert D. Miller, Repints: Extra Copies
Review Copy of Capillary Thermodynamics in Serially Porous Media, with Implications for Randomly Porous Media
Agronomy Course 607 R.D. Miller-Overheads-Soil Physics Part 1 Statics
Soil Particles, Mechanical Analysis, Representative Samples, Textual Triangle, Closely Packed Spheres
Water Contend, Bulk Density, Neutron Probe
Forces, Ideal Gas Law, Properties of Water, Static Equilibrium in Force Field
Surface Tension, Capillary Rice, Bubbles, Phase Barriers
Wetting and Drying, Hysteresis, Air Entry, Independent Domains
Haines and Fisher Analyses, Terzaghi Equation, Aitchison's Concept, Synder's Work
Clay Minerals, Electrophoresis and Electroosmosis, Helmholz-Schmolvchowski Equation, Ideal Diffuse Electrical Double Layer, Dispersion
Agronomy Course 607 R.D. Miller-Overheads-Soil Physics Part 2 Dynamics
Recapitulation, Component Potentials, Adsorption Versus Capillary Space
Flow in Saturated Soils, Darcy's Law, Kozeny Equations, Geometric Mean Diameter
Method of Images, Electrostatic Analogue, Flow Nets, Dupuit Equation
Flow in Unsaturated Soils, Equation of Continuity, Diffusivity, Available Water Range
Scale Modelling, Grain, Substance and Forle Analogues, Scaling Statics, Scaling Dynamics
Coupling of Transport Processes, Frost Heave, Freezing and Thawing Versus Wetting and Drying, Schofield's Work
Agronomy Course 607 R.D. Miller, Course Materials
Capillary Conductivity of A Plate Perforated with Cylindrical Holes
Draft of A Revision to Hillel

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Prepared as an addendum to a revised version of Hillel's Soil and Water, Physical Principles and Processes

did.unittitle: Scientific work and lectures from Russia trip

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Reports on thruway, pipeline and cold region soil heaving projects

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Skits and speeches written, namely for retirements of staff in the Agronomy Department. Includes items regarding Mark Barlow, Marlin Cline, Mike Peech, Ed Lemon, Bob Musgrave, Earl Stone, Paul Zwerman, Wright, Brady, Aldrich, others. Also audio tapes (tr-10890 Zwerman, Drosdoff; tr-10891 Lemon; tr-10892 Inceptisol, Peech, Zwerman, Drosdoff) and slides of these speeches.

did.unittitle: Secondary Heaving HRB, Loch, Torrance, Cass, Hoekstra, Koopmans
Doctoral Students' Slides, Work, Scaling, RDM's Slides
Field Photos, Caldwell F, Taber, Beskow, Miller Baker Kiolian, Römkens
Phase Barrier, Massive Ice, O'NGM; Sunder, Scaling
P.Black, Horiguichi, Perfect, Massive Ice, Seminar 1981
Permafrost III. Edmanton, Canada, Pictures of Experimental Apparatus, Pictures of Freezing-related Phenomena

scopecontent.p: Slides

scopecontent.p: Slides

did.unittitle: Forms from the U.S.S.R. Embassy regarding trip to Russia

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs from trips to Europe, including Germany, Munich, Heidelberg, Oberwesel, Burg Schonburg, Dresden, Nordlingen, Frankfurt, Dinklesbuhl, Austria, Innsbruch, France, Verdun, England, Stonehenge, Scotland, Edinburgh, golfing, Ireland, Lyons Kansas salt mine, geoogy field trip, Boulder Colorado, Bill Cargill, Dr. Bronson, Wyoming, William C. Warnerson, possibly University of Missouri, family photos, tap day,

did.unittitle: Photo Album of Negatives from European trip
Certificate of Appreciation - The Federal Inspector
Faculty-Alumni Award - Alumni Association of the University of Missouri-Columbia
Recognition of Outstanding Achievement in Agronomy - American Society of Agronomy Northeastern Branch
Certificats from American Society of Agronomy, Soil Science Society of America
Series IX. Personal and Family Materials

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The papers of Prof. Miller's father Merritt Finley Miller are in the University of Missouri archives and the Missouri State Historical Society archives

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This is Prof. Miller's high school. The folder includes his correspondence with the school, speech to 1961 class, and a German song book.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Humorous (re: neighbor's dog), letter re '82 trip to Moscow, notes and account of '73 trip to Siberia, Retirement of Nile Brady-Sketch

did.unittitle: Article on Robert D. Miller and Edward E. Miller: "The Agronomy Brothers"
Family History, Newspaper copy of a report on Edward Mix, First Cousin of Merritt F. Miller
East-Central Illinois Landforms and Ecosystems in the Making 20,000 Years by Elisabeth M. Hanson

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Elisabeth Hanson is Prof. Miller's sister.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Records family's 1933-34 sabbatical trip to Germany throughout Europe, kept by Prof. Miller's mother Flora Grace Ernst Miller. Front page picture drawn by youthful Prof. Miller

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Records family's 1933-34 sabbatical trip to Germany throughout Europe.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Records family's 1933-34 sabbatical trip to Germany throughout Europe.

did.unittitle: Letter from Sam Walton, the founder of Walmart
A Photograph of Obama, with the Signature of Joe Biden Printed
Statement of Elisabeth Hanson (Prof. Muller's sister) regarding Being on the Andrea Doria
Autobiography of Carl Julius Ernest (Prof. Miller's Maternal Grandfather)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Carl Julius Ernst's Papers are in the Nebraska Historical Society

did.unittitle: Copy of Note Scrap and Remark Book of Edward Eddy Miller (Prof. Miller's Grandfather), with Prof. Miller's Introduction

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: E.E.Miler served in Ohio regiment. The original is in Ohio Historical Society collection.

did.unittitle: Prof. Miller's Address and Appointment Books 1-3
A Small Electrical Piece of Lab Equipment with A Brass Part Machined by Prof. Miller

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photos as a baby, wedding photo, family photos, photos from Cornell, image of Miller being interviewed by Bob Simon during Cornell 69.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 1966 family letter, files regarding employment, passports, letter to the editor about Jack Musick, pamphlet "The Story of an Emigrant Boy", awards, invite to a cocktail party from Adlai Stevenson, University of Missouri 1940 diploma, account book kept by Miller 1935-1936, Grandparent book describing the life or Miller's sister Elisabeth Marie Miller describing her life which is informative about Robert's early life as well, birth certificate, resumes, Ithaca Journal article about potholes, envelope containing keys that may have belonged to his office in Caldwell Hall.

did.unittitle: The University in Transition by James A. Perkins

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inscribed to Miller by Perkins.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: David h. Hickman High School yearbook, annotated by Miller and classmates.

did.unittitle: Prof. Miller's Address and Appointment Books
Scrapbook from Junior and High School, and College

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Items from Jefferson Junior High School Columbia Missouri, Hickman High School, programs, report cards, photographs, Boy Scouts of America cards, University of Missouri items, Barnwarmin' programs, Alpha Zeta material, correspondence.

did.unittitle: Series X. World War II Materials
Materials Prepared for the Book Descent from Niitaka
Memorandum and Agreement of Descant from Niitaka, Responses Received after the Publication
Correspondence in Search of Information and Responses
Material Prepared for History Classes at Ithaca High School

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Explosion of American B 24 bomber, taken by Prof. Miller beside foxhole

did.unittitle: World War II and Military Materials

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes orders, correspondence about his memorabilia with museums, photographs, sketches, correspondence with the Veterans Administration and regarding his lectures to high schools. Also a letter written to the Committee on the Armed Services from April 3, 1993 on Miller's thoughts on gays in the military.

did.unittitle: CDs of photographs related to WWII

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Niitaka photos (raising flag, Nagasaki), Interview Circus, Museum Presentation (D-Day)
